contents.value: **def** — Clojure keyword.
**defn** — Clojure keyword.
**defmacro** — Clojure keyword.
**defmulti** — Clojure keyword.
**defmethod** — Clojure keyword.
**println** — Built-in function/command in Clojure.
**print** — Built-in function/command in Clojure.
**str** — Built-in function/command in Clojure.
**map** — Built-in function/command in Clojure.
**filter** — Built-in function/command in Clojure.
A **class** defines a blueprint for objects with fields and methods.
An **interface** declares method signatures without implementation.
An **abstract** class cannot be instantiated and may contain abstract methods.
**final** prevents inheritance (class), overriding (method), or reassignment (variable).
**static** members belong to the class rather than instances.
**void** indicates a method returns no value.
**extends** creates a subclass that inherits from a superclass.
**implements** declares that a class fulfills an interface contract.

**throw** raises an exception.
```java
throw new IllegalArgumentException("msg");
```

**try-catch** handles exceptions gracefully.
```java
try { ... } catch (Exception e) { ... } finally { ... }
```

**Generics** let you parameterize types.
```java
List<String> items = new ArrayList<>();
```

**Annotations** provide metadata about code.
```java
@Override, @Deprecated, @SuppressWarnings
```

A **lambda** is an anonymous function expression.
```java
(a, b) -> a + b
```

**Streams** provide functional-style operations on collections.
```java
list.stream().filter(x -> x > 0).collect(Collectors.toList())
```
A **package** organizes related classes into namespaces.

```clojure
(let [bindings*] body)
```
Creates local bindings in a lexical scope. Takes a vector of binding pairs (name value) and a body of expressions. Bindings are sequential—later bindings can reference earlier ones. The value of the last expression in the body is returned.

```clojure
(fn [params*] body)
```
Creates an anonymous (lambda) function. Takes a vector of parameters and a body of expressions. Supports multi-arity definitions and can optionally include a name for self-reference in recursive calls. Used extensively for callbacks, map functions, and closures.

```clojure
(reduce f init coll) -> value
```
Reduces the collection to a single value by repeatedly applying function f to an accumulator and each element. If init is supplied, it serves as the initial accumulator; otherwise the first two elements are used. Returns the final accumulated value after processing all elements.

```clojure
(atom x) -> Atom
```
Creates an Atom, a thread-safe mutable reference type that provides synchronous, uncoordinated, independent state management. Atoms ensure atomic updates via compare-and-swap semantics and are the primary way to manage shared mutable state in Clojure programs.

```clojure
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```clojure
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```clojure
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```clojure
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```clojure
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```clojure
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```clojure
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```clojure
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
